for_verification_agent.acceptance_criteria: Kubernetes manifests exist under deploy/k8s/ for all 4 services (translating deploy/docker-compose.yml's wiring, not a rewrite), locally validated against a real kind/k3d cluster (pods Ready, /health and / endpoints reachable), plus a documented (not executed) OCI provisioning path.

how_to_reproduce_the_kind_validation.commands: See deploy/k8s/README.md's 'Local validation (kind)' section verbatim -- brew install kind (if absent); docker build the 4 images with :kindtest tag; kind create cluster --name <name>; kind load docker-image x4; kubectl apply -f deploy/k8s/; kubectl wait --for=condition=Ready pod --all -n hivemind --timeout=90s; kubectl port-forward svc/api and svc/ui, curl /health and /; kind delete cluster.
how_to_reproduce_the_kind_validation.expected: All 4 pods (agents, api, engine, ui) reach 1/1 Ready within ~20s. curl .../health returns 'ok'. curl .../ contains '<div id="root">'.

key_design_decisions_to_check: architecture-discovery.md decisions 1-11 -- especially: compose service names kept identical to k8s Service names (DNS drop-in), no depends_on-equivalent used (api/agents/ui's own clients tolerate late-ready dependencies, no initContainers added), HEALTHCHECK -> readiness/liveness probes (tcpSocket for engine/agents, httpGet for api/ui), ephemeral emptyDir for engine's /data (no new persistence guarantee beyond what compose had), plain manifests chosen over Helm per user's stated preference.

deploy/k8s/agents-secret.example.yaml is applied as-is by `kubectl apply -f deploy/k8s/` (creates an empty-valued Secret, envFrom.secretRef marked optional:true on the agents Deployment) -- this is intentional, mirrors compose's ${VAR:-} blank-default behavior, documented in deploy/k8s/README.md and self-consistency.json's known_caveat_not_a_defect.

deploy/oci/provision.sh is explicitly, prominently marked UNVERIFIED AGAINST A LIVE OCI ACCOUNT (no OCI credentials in this sandbox) -- verify the disclosure is present and honest, not that the OCI steps actually work (they cannot be tested here).
No message queue / new architecture component introduced anywhere -- confirm via diff that only k8s-object + doc files were added/changed, no engine/api/agents/ui source touched.

what_was_actually_executed_vs_documented.executed_for_real: kind cluster creation, image build+load, kubectl apply, pod-Ready wait, port-forward + curl /health and / -- done twice for reproducibility, both clean PASS + clean teardown.
what_was_actually_executed_vs_documented.documented_only_not_executed: deploy/oci/provision.sh's OCI CLI calls -- no OCI account/credentials available in this sandbox, disclosed prominently in the script header and deploy/oci/README.md.

self_consistency_summary.overall: PASS (internal sanity only, not verification -- see .cdr/runs/2026-07-12/025-implementation/self-consistency.json for all 10 matrix items)
self_consistency_summary.kind_validation_result: PASS -- all 4 pods reached Ready, api /health returned 'ok', ui / served the SPA shell, both via a live kind cluster, reproduced twice, torn down cleanly both times

docs_pointers.hld: docs/HLD.md repo-layout deploy/ line updated (no longer '(not yet built)')

not_in_scope_this_subtask: Actually provisioning any real OCI compute/account resources -- no credentials in this sandbox
Implementing api's /graph, /files, /admin Go handlers (pre-existing gap, unrelated)
